item.title: Re: Image attribution
item.content:encoded: I don't want to say this, but this image is under Non-Commercial license, so it's not available on SCP wiki. I think you should find another one.

item.title: Image attribution
item.content:encoded:
As it turns out, the image on this article may have not been in CC. Replaced it with a new one.

Image belongs to Shadow Chasers on Flickr, borrowed under CC 2.0 license.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: timer expired, edits made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

This SCP mentions Site-88, which has a dossier now. I propose linking the two.

Editing this:

Each SCP-1774 instance is to be kept in Class-2 Spectral Containment units in separate wings of Site 88.

To this:

Each SCP-1774 instance is to be kept in Class-2 Spectral Containment units in separate wings of Site 88.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hah, really enjoyed this one, +1 no question :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. I've always said I'd mess with people if I were a ghost, and it's kind of funny to see that concept applied to fiction. Random skip button, you deliver to me unseen greatness once again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well written, and the meta-ness of this is amazing. I would like to upvote all of the upvotes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
To quote wikipedia:

IR film is sensitive to infrared (IR) radiation in the 250°C to 500°C range, while the range of thermography is approximately −50°C to over 2,000°C.

From what I understood, infrared cameras are what ghost hunting shows use for their "normal" cameras, an thermographic cameras are the ones that pick up heat signatures. but I may be completely incorrect on this. If so, I await my flogging.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I stand corrected.
Huh, now I want to know too.

Hey, Ihpkm! What's the deal?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: On a reread, I like this a lot more than I did at first. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
'Current extant pieces include a handheld infrared video camera (SCP-1774-01), a handheld themographic video camera (SCP-1774-02)'

Nope, there's an infrared video camera and a thermographic video camera.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Haversham ghost"? Do I spot a Dickens reference?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a thermographic camera and an infrared thermometer, not two types of cameras.
The thermometer would be something like one of these.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love it! There's only one thing, I'm not clear on the difference between an infrared camera and a thermographic camera. Aren't all infrared cameras thermographic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think that's pretty strange phrasing.
Oh well, might just be me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fun and creative. I like it. :} +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why would censoring that be necessary?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know it's not against the rules, but it breaks immersion for me. In my headcanon, most people viewing these articles are Level 3 at the most, and the only people who see the entire unabridged logs are the O5s, the Ethics Committee, and a handful of very very trusted Level 5 researchers. So yeah, personal bias prevents me from upvoting this article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Termination" is a specific euphemism for when the Foundation deliberately executes someone. You can't "accidentally terminate" something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is pretty brilliant, and the test logs are great. I especially loved "Further attempts at communication resulted in sounds of sobbing, laughter, and the statement "I love this job."" What a guy.

That said, I thought the interview just sort of told us everything we already knew from the article. It's not bad, but it also doesn't seem all that important. Certainly didn't ruin the article or anything!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's not against the rules to redact things. I use real-world names and junk all the time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this needs a little more censorship. SCP documents don't often specifically name people or locations (I.E. Ohio State University).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I particularly like the interview log. McGee really sounds like an agent trying to sound like a cop and only getting it mostly right. It's really hard to do a character who is doing a sub-character, without slipping over and just doing the second level entirely. Good job.

My only nitpick is the test numbering.
"Test 1774-01" is much too similar to "SCP-1774-01", the whole point of having naming conventions is to remove ambiguity and possible sources of miscommunication. Maybe make them "Test #1" instead?

I don't know if the change is worth it (it's obviously perfectly enjoyable as-is), but it did jump out at me while reading.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The concept is quite good and is well-executed, with the exception of the interview log, which has one or two amusing bits of dialogue but otherwise feels too artificially expository. Not enough to keep me from an upvote, but somewhat irritatingly so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good concept spiced with irony. I can't ask for nothing better than that. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd be interested to know if the Foundation followed through and checked out this method to "bind spirits."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Of course, leave it to a bunch of Ohio State students to do the most convoluted method of getting some ghosts for a paranormal investigations. This is why the Wolverines are better in every respect: we have honor when faking our paranormal stuff.

Ihpkmn, if you're a Buckeye, I meant nothing by it man. I'm just messin' with ya. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm guessing he meant a cold-spot detected in the shape of a humanoid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this as a draft, and I still like it now. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but one question:
What do you mean by "one humanoid 'cold spot'?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …that's actually an artifact from an old version of the article. I've fixed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this one. +1

But what happened in Test 19 referenced in the containment procedures?

item.title: Faking Ghosts With Ghosts
item.content:encoded:
I like this one, but I feel like it could be tightened up still.

to ensure that the entities contained within are not mistakenly terminated.

Something sounds off to me about this line. I'm nitpicking here but "mistakenly" doesn't sound very official to me. Maybe "terminated due to oversight" or something?

SCP-1774 is the collective designation for several pieces of equipment formerly used by an amateur paranormal investigation agency.

I know you come out and say the agency later on in the read, but it again strikes me as odd that they aren't properly identified here since this is the first reference to them.

I like the test logs but the interview log is too on the nose IMO. It just seems to give any and all mystery away with the whole, "I found this thing online where you could bind spirits to anything you wanted, make a haunted box or something." If there's a site that says that, doesn't that already beat them to the point of proving that ghosts exist? Both of the subjects in the log seem too straight forward about it all.

Anyways, I upvoted but I don't care for the current interview log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really liked this article as I once watched Ghost Hunters myself. It was an interesting take on the concept of how they are just recording fake activity. However, was wondering if you should add more censoring/blank out things such as the agent's name, or the location of retrieval?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A good take on ghosts. I found the whole thing to be a good read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Parts of the interview seemed redundant. Other than than, quite good.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The test logs really sold this for me, and the interview pushed this from "pretty okay" to "pretty damn good".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I got a kick out of the whole "prove paranormal entities exist by faking paranormal evidence premise." I feel like it could use more depth though

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Blame Ghost Hunters for this article. I was watching it for some reason, and thought, Wouldn't it be trippy if the ghosts were real, but actually helping to fake the evidence?

Anyway, enjoy!
